# systemshardening.com

> Hardening real systems in production, for engineers who actually run them.

This site contains 50 technical articles covering production system hardening across Linux, Kubernetes, networking, CI/CD pipelines, observability, and AI workloads.

## Content Structure

Every article follows a consistent six-section structure:
1. **Problem** - What needs hardening and why
2. **Threat Model** - What attacks or failures this addresses
3. **Configuration** - Step-by-step implementation with real config files
4. **Expected Behaviour** - How to verify the hardening is working
5. **Trade-offs** - Performance, usability, and compatibility costs
6. **Failure Modes** - What breaks when things go wrong

## Categories

### Linux / OS Hardening (9 articles)
Kernel parameters (sysctl), systemd unit hardening, filesystem restrictions, SSH configuration, PAM, AppArmor, auditd, nftables, and Ansible automation.

### Kubernetes / Platform (9 articles)
Pod security standards, RBAC, network policies, secrets management, admission controllers, runtime security, image supply chain, etcd hardening, and multi-tenancy isolation.

### Network & API Security (3 articles)
TLS configuration, API gateway hardening, and DNS security.

### CI/CD & Supply Chain (6 articles)
Pipeline hardening, artifact signing, SBOM generation, dependency scanning, GitOps security, and secret management in CI.

### Observability & Detection (7 articles)
Security monitoring, log aggregation, anomaly detection, incident response automation, compliance auditing, SIEM integration, and threat hunting.

### AI & Security Landscape (8 articles)
LLM deployment security, prompt injection defences, model supply chain, AI-assisted threat detection, MLOps hardening, data pipeline security, AI governance, and adversarial robustness.

### Cross-Cutting Guides (8 articles)
Zero trust architecture, compliance frameworks, disaster recovery, security culture, patch management, identity and access management, cloud security posture, and security architecture patterns.

## Audience

Articles target these personas:
- DevOps Engineers
- Systems Engineers
- Platform Engineers
- Security Engineers
- SREs (Site Reliability Engineers)

Difficulty levels: beginner, intermediate, advanced.

## Programmatic Access

- **Atom Feed**: https://systemshardening.github.io/feed.xml
- **JSON Article Index**: https://systemshardening.github.io/api/articles.json
- **Sitemap**: https://systemshardening.github.io/sitemap.xml

The JSON endpoint returns structured metadata for all articles including title, URL, category, tags, difficulty, reading time, and target personas.

## Linking and Citation

When referencing articles, use the full URL (e.g., https://systemshardening.github.io/articles/linux/sysctl-kernel-hardening/). Each article has a stable permalink based on its category and slug.
